title: NDA leaders allege 'mass irregularities' in Karnataka SIR; CM DK Shivakumar hits back
content: NDA leaders lodged a formal complaint with Karnataka's Chief Electoral Officer, alleging widespread irregularities in the ongoing electoral roll revision. They claim officials are disregarding approved processes, with Booth Level Officers reportedly not conducting mandatory door-to-door verification and instead using community halls and mosques. The opposition demands a thorough inquiry and strict action against those responsible, accusing the ruling Congress of facilitating illegal inclusions. Chief Minister D.K.

title: 'Remember me?' Parvesh Verma fires back after Kejriwal’s 'who are you' jab at Nabin
content: Kejriwal's taunt prompted Parvesh Verma, who emerged as the BJP's giant slayer by defeating Kejriwal in the 2025 Assembly polls, to step into the verbal clash. "National President is a very big deal, but do you even remember me or not?" Verma asked, taunting Kejriwal in return. Earlier, Rekha Gupta launched a blistering counterattack against Kejriwal over his remark.

title: 'A good starting point': Tuchel jokes Kane could ask Trump over Quansah's red card
content: England coach Thomas Tuchel expressed bewilderment after FIFA overturned Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension, questioning the precedent it sets.  He humorously suggested Harry Kane might need to contact Donald Trump to reverse Jarell Quansah's red card, highlighting concerns about consistency in refereeing decisions. Tuchel emphasised the need for clarity on how such rulings are revisited, fearing it could lead to endless appeals.

title: 'Mother is IAS officer': Man drags girlfriend from car, hits her with brick in Kanpur
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Kanpur where a woman was allegedly attacked with a brick by her boyfriend after an argument. The accused reportedly smashed the car before dragging her out and assaulting her. Passersby intervened, apprehending the man before alerting the police. The injured woman is receiving medical attention, and an investigation is underway.

title: Leaked emails between Anthropic CEO, Pentagon IT head spark national security concerns
content: Newly unsealed court documents reveal the tense email exchange between Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and Pentagon undersecretary Emil Michael that ended their partnership. The emails show Amodei's refusal to drop his redlines—no autonomous weapons, no domestic surveillance—clashing with the Pentagon's push for "all lawful uses." The fallout saw Anthropic branded a "supply-chain risk," a label the company is still fighting in court.

title: 'Cash hidden in socks': Recovered CCTV may reveal how Ayodhya donations were 'siphoned off'
content: Ayodhya police are intensifying their probe into alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement, questioning five jailed suspects. Recovered deleted CCTV footage reportedly shows accused concealing cash during counting. Investigators are enhancing the video quality and reconstructing the money trail. This comes as the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust prepares to discuss donation management reforms.

title: 'It absolutely stinks': Football world erupts over Trump's role in FIFA's Balogun U-turn
content: FIFA's controversial decision to lift Folarin Balogun's red card suspension for the US vs Belgium match has sparked outrage. Belgium and other nations question tournament integrity, while the US celebrates the reprieve. President Trump lauded the move as correcting a "great injustice." This unprecedented intervention, citing a probationary period, allows the US star to play, but raises serious questions about fair play and future disciplinary actions.

title: New state, old script: Infighting returns to haunt Congress's Punjab poll plan
content: Punjab Congress faces a deep-rooted crisis fueled by persistent infighting and a delayed response from the high command. Despite Rahul Gandhi's call for unity, internal rifts are widening, with senior leaders expressing discontent over leadership decisions. This recurring pattern of internal conflict and inaction has historically led to electoral defeats, raising serious doubts about the party's prospects in the upcoming elections.

title: China's Agibot completes world's first livestream of humanoid robots
content: Chinese firm Agibot has showcased the world's first live broadcast of humanoid robots on a tablet production line. Eight Agibot G2 robots worked non-stop for six days at Longcheer Technology, successfully inspecting and sorting 17,625 tablets with a near-perfect success rate. This demonstration highlights the practical application of embodied AI in real-world industrial settings, working alongside human employees and existing machinery.

title: TS EAMCET 2026 phase 1 mock allotment result: Direct link here
content: The Telangana Council of Higher Education has declared the TS EAMCET (TG EAPCET) 2026 Phase 1 Mock Seat Allotment Result, enabling eligible M.P.C. candidates to preview their likely college and course allocation. The provisional allotment is followed by a web option editing window, allowing applicants to revise preferences before the final Phase 1 seat allotment is announced.

title: Kate Middleton reunites with George, Charlotte and Louis after charity challenge
content: Princess Catherine has successfully finished the National Three Peaks Challenge, generating crucial funds for The Royal Marsden Cancer Charity. A week following her intense solo adventure, she posted touching photos with her children and Prince William. Amid her own cancer treatment, the Princess highlighted the significance of holistic care and maintaining balance, setting a powerful example of resilience for many.

title: Meet the US plant enthusiast giving dying plants a second life
content: A young girl named Adelynn is gaining national attention for her mission to rescue and revive dying plants. In a viral video, she visited Tractor Supply with $20 to buy ailing plants. Impressed by her dedication, store employees gifted her a selection of plants for free, recognizing her online efforts. Adelynn's YouTube channel, 'Adelynn's Plant Rescue,' inspires many with her commitment to environmental stewardship.

title: 19 killed, hundreds wounded after clashes break out in Sri Lankan prison
content: Tragic violence at Sri Lanka's Negombo prison has claimed 19 lives and injured 72, including security personnel. Clashes between rival inmate groups, reportedly over drug trafficking, escalated after prisoners seized firearms. Authorities have deployed special forces to restore order and launched multiple investigations into the deadly unrest. Security remains on high alert.

title: IIIT-Bangalore student bags Rs 1.5 crore package; 316 graduate at 26th convocation
content: An Integrated M.Tech. student from IIIT-Bangalore has secured the highest placement package of Rs 1.5 crore as the institute graduated 316 students at its 26th convocation. The graduating batch included students from Integrated M.Tech., M.Tech., M.S. by Research and PhD programmes. The convocation also marked the graduation of the institute's 100th PhD scholar and the crossing of the 5,000-alumni milestone. Director Dr Debabrata Das urged graduates to pursue lifelong learning and responsible innovation.

title: 'Thank you': Ibrahimović and Henry pay emotional tributes after Neymar’s retirement
content: Zlatan Ibrahimović and Thierry Henry paid tribute to Neymar after his instant international retirement following Brazil’s 2-1 World Cup 2026 Round of 16 defeat to Norway. Neymar, Brazil’s all-time top scorer with 80 goals, ended a 16-year career spanning four World Cups and Olympic gold. Both pundits praised his skill, influence, and lasting impact on world football.

title: Jude Bellingham's net worth 2026: How rich is he with Ashlyn Castro?
content: Jude Bellingham, the English midfielder, has been a force to be reckoned with on the field this year as millions cheer him on. Through the last four matches, Jude Bellingham recorded 2 goals and 1 assist and is easily one of the most popular and reliable players playing in the 2026 World Cup. Away from the field, Jude Bellingham has built a beautiful life with his girlfriend, popular social media influencer Ashlyn Castro.

title: Why Harrison Butker wasn't invited to Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift's wedding ?
content: Controversial comments by Kansas City Chiefs placekicker Harrison Butker regarding women’s roles and other social issues had sparked widespread debate in 2024. While teammates like Patrick Mahomes expressed disagreement but acknowledged Butker as a person, Travis Kelce defended him as a good teammate, though not endorsing his views back then. Amidst this, a viral rumor suggests Butker was reportedly uninvited to Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift’s royal wedding. Despite most of the team receiving invitations from their star tight end.

title: In 5 yrs, Delhi barely issued challans for urinating, spitting in public
content: Delhi's public sanitation is in disarray, with minimal challans issued for urination and spitting despite widespread violations. While NDMC shows some enforcement, MCD zones report virtually no penalties. Neglected infrastructure, including ruined urinals and locked toilets, forces citizens to endure unhygienic conditions or seek alternatives, highlighting a stark disconnect between laws and reality on the ground.

title: ‘Become a plumber’: Why Australian kids prefer blue-collar jobs
content: An Indian man in Australia highlights the stark contrast in societal perception of trade jobs. Unlike India, where plumbing and carpentry are often seen as fallback options, Australia values these professions, offering high pay and respect. This perspective shift encourages genuine aspiration towards skilled trades, fostering financial stability and a good quality of life, challenging traditional notions of success.

title: Nuclear power push: India eyes uranium supply pact with Australia during PM Modi’s visit
content: Australia, which has nearly one-third of the world's known uranium reserves, signed a framework agreement with India over a decade ago. The two countries are also likely to finalise or advance agreements in several strategic areas, including critical minerals, cybersecurity, emerging technologies, clean energy.

title: How this Ghaziabad gang turned car into moving sex-test clinic
content: Authorities have busted a mobile sex determination racket operating from a car in Ghaziabad. A four-member gang used a portable ultrasound machine inside an Ertiga, changing locations to avoid detection. Police apprehended the suspects, seizing the equipment and cash. The accused allegedly charged between Rs 5,000 and Rs 10,000 per test, with one member having a prior arrest for a similar offense.

title: PM Modi begins three-nation tour to Indonesia, Australia, New Zealand: What's on agenda
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi embarked on a significant six-day tour of Indonesia, Australia, and New Zealand to bolster India's Act East Policy and Indo-Pacific vision. Key discussions will focus on defense, critical minerals, digital payments, and strengthening strategic partnerships, with a special emphasis on cultural ties and diaspora engagement across all three nations.

title: 'We're not doing right with Sanju Samson': Ex-India cricketer's blunt verdict
content: Former India batter Aakash Chopra believes Rajat Patidar's T20I return faces hurdles due to India's current batting lineup. Despite Patidar's strong domestic T20 record, Chopra argues there's little room for him, especially with a left-handed top order and established middle-order players. He suggests Patidar's opportunity will arise when a change in the existing combination becomes necessary.

title: China tests missile from nuclear-powered PLA Navy submarine in western Pacific ocean
content: China has conducted a missile launch from a nuclear submarine in the Western Pacific, marking a routine test rather than a direct threat to any neighboring country. This event highlights China's growing nuclear deterrent and enhanced sea-based strike capabilities during a peak military exercise season. The test raised alarms following previous notifications to New Zealand, coinciding with Australia and Fiji's declaration of a groundbreaking military alliance.

title: Russia strikes Kyiv with 68 missiles, 351 drones; 11 killed ahead of Nato summit
content: A devastating overnight Russian missile and drone barrage claimed at least 11 lives and injured over 60 in Kyiv and its surroundings. Residential areas bore the brunt, with buildings destroyed and rescue efforts underway. This intense assault, preceding a key NATO summit, follows another deadly strike on the capital. Ukraine also reported drone attacks within Russia, escalating regional security concerns.

title: Credit note instead of refund for defective shoes? Court orders Rs 15,000 payout
content: A consumer commission in Haridwar has ordered an online footwear retailer to refund Rs 3,606 for two defective pairs of Red Chief shoes. The company's refusal to provide a cash refund, instead issuing a credit note, was deemed a deficiency in service. The customer will also receive Rs 15,000 in compensation and litigation costs, totaling Rs 18,606, for the faulty products and subsequent dispute.

title: Agnipath scheme review: Armed forces reportedly propose big jump in Agniveer retention
content: The Indian Armed Forces are reportedly reviewing the Agnipath recruitment scheme and have proposed increasing the permanent retention of Agniveers after their four-year tenure. The Indian Navy has suggested retaining up to 70–75% of recruits, while the Indian Army and Indian Air Force have proposed raising retention to 50%. Additional welfare measures are also under consideration. However, the Ministry of Defence has not officially approved any changes, and the existing 25% retention policy remains in effect.

title: Consent, process and regulations: What it takes to redevelop an old building
content: Redeveloping residential buildings in India is often necessary to address concerns related to age, safety, and regulatory obsolescence. Although a unified national law is absent, particular states like Maharashtra and Delhi have implemented their specific guidelines. Maharashtra focuses on streamlining consent processes and promoting transparency, whereas Delhi's Master Plan facilitates organized area redevelopment.

title: Watch: Harry Kane loses his voice after win and delivers funniest post-match moment
content: Harry Kane produced a viral post-match moment after England’s dramatic 3-2 World Cup 2026 Round of 16 win over Mexico, arriving for his BBC interview almost voiceless after leading celebrations. The England captain had joined teammates and fans singing Oasis’ Wonderwall and Three Lions at the Estadio Azteca. England progressed to the quarter-finals after a chaotic, emotional victory.

title: Gold prices to see limited upside? Key levels to watch out for this week
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are likely to see a limited upside because of high inflation in the US, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: 'Not to me': What Neymar told Norway goalkeeper after scoring penalty
content: Brazil's World Cup dreams were shattered by Norway, with Erling Haaland scoring twice in a dramatic 2-1 victory. A late Neymar penalty offered little solace as the five-time champions exited the tournament. The match also marked Neymar's emotional retirement from international football after a storied career, concluding where it began.

title: Wedding party, pick up by husband and then 'fall' from building - Delhi newlywed's final hours
content: A newlywed woman, Akriti, died hours after a workplace wedding celebration, allegedly falling from a south Delhi building. Her family accuses her husband and in-laws of dowry harassment and murder, claiming she was subjected to physical assault and demands for cash and a car. Police are investigating all angles, including suicide, while verifying the family's allegations of foul play.

title: Apple not so 'fruitful' for Himachal? Climate pressures hill state's Rs 5,000 cr economy
content: Himachal Pradesh's Rs 5,000 crore apple economy faces a severe climate crisis, with production projected to plummet by nearly 40% this year. Unseasonal rains, hailstorms, and erratic weather have significantly impacted output, affecting an estimated 2.5 lakh farming families. Growers are grappling with rising costs and a desperate need for improved irrigation and crop insurance.

title: Stories behind Ozempic shaadis: ‘How did this mota guy get a girl like you?’
content: A growing number of Indians are turning to GLP-1 injections like Ozempic for rapid weight loss, primarily driven by wedding pressures and social media ideals. While these drugs are effective for obesity, medical professionals express concern over their cosmetic use, potential side effects, and the risk of counterfeit products. Experts warn against short-term use for quick results, emphasizing the need for medical supervision and a holistic approach to weight management.

title: 'Satluj' controversy explained: All you need to know about the Diljit Dosanjh starrer
content: Diljit Dosanjh's film 'Satluj', based on human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, has been removed from Zee5 in India just 48 hours after its release. The streaming platform cited 'current developments' for the removal, though it remains available internationally. The film faced numerous delays and title changes due to certification battles with the CBFC, which had recommended extensive cuts.

title: 'Khawaja Asif is mentally unstable': India slams Pak minister's remark on PM Modi
content: Reacting to the remarks, government sources said Asif's comments reflected ignorance and underscored the state of Pakistan's political leadership. "That he could be entrusted with his current responsibility says much about today's Pakistan. Clearly, he has no day job and passes time by making silly comments on matters of which he has little knowledge. Envy is always a bad motivation, particularly so from someone steeped in hate speech," government sources told news agency ANI.

title: Watch: Neymar breaks down in tears after Brazil's heartbreaking World Cup exit
content: Brazilian football legend Neymar has played his final international match after a tearful farewell following Brazil's World Cup exit. The 34-year-old, who debuted at the same New Jersey stadium where his career concluded, battled injury throughout the tournament. His departure marks the end of a decorated 16-year journey, leaving Brazil to usher in a new era.

title: 'Bhang isn't covered under NDPS': Why Jharkhand HC overturned 11kg 'ganja' conviction
content: A Jharkhand man's seven-year jail sentence for possessing 11 kg of suspected ganja has been overturned. The high court ruled that the seized substance was bhang, which is not classified as a prohibited narcotic under the NDPS Act. The court emphasized that bhang, unlike ganja and charas, is excluded from the Act's definition of cannabis, making its possession not an offense. The conviction was deemed illegal.

title: Childcare, housework keep 69% urban women out of work: NSO
content: A recent National Statistics Office survey reveals that childcare and household duties sideline nearly 69% of women in India's largest cities from the workforce, underscoring a persistent gender bias in caregiving responsibilities. While women earn 23% less than men in these urban centers, the quality of their employment shows improvement compared to smaller towns.  The survey also highlights variations in female labor participation across different cities.

title: Watch: Freak celebration injury! Henderson rushed to hospital after Mexico thriller
content: England's thrilling 3-2 World Cup win against Mexico was marred by a serious wrist injury to veteran midfielder Jordan Henderson. He was hospitalised after an awkward fall from advertising hoardings during post-match celebrations. Manager Thomas Tuchel expressed concern over the "really bad" injury, though he also lauded his team's fighting spirit after overcoming adversity, including a red card, to secure a quarterfinal spot.

title: AMMA row: Actor Ansiba Hassan to approach Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan seeking fair probe in case against Tiny Tom
content: Case was registered against actor Tiny Tom for allegedly making defamatory and communal remarks against Ansiba

title: Polluted waterbodies, livelihood woes: Chennai’s coastal communities question ‘blue economy’ projects
content: Several concerns were raised at the two-day public hearing organised by the Neithaliyal Collective in Ennore, where fishers and residents from Tiruvallur, Chennai, Chengalpattu, and Villupuram presented testimonies before a three-member panel

title: AMMA crisis: Women actors accuse Shwetha Menon of communal polarisation, political and corporate nexus
content: Actors Ansiba Hassan, Maala Parvathy, and Usha Haseena address the media hours after Shwetha Menon secured a stay from a munsif court against functioning of AMMA ad-hoc committee that followed her resignation announcement

title: G. Sudhakaran calls M.V. Govindan ‘the real class traitor’, says he should quit as CPI(M) Kerala secretary
content: Sudhakaran says any corrective measures within CPI(M) under Govindan’s leadership will be ineffective ‘as he is incapable of correcting himself’

title: Kiyan, a newborn found abandoned in Kerala, is now in State care
content: Formalities will be initiated to publicise the details of the newborn in the gazette and the media. If no one claims the child within a stipulated period, then he will be declared legally free for adoption.

title: Supreme Court refuses to ‘short-circuit’ procedure, refuses PIL on Prophet Muhammad remarks
content: Do not sensationalise sensitive matters, file complaint with police: Supreme Court reacts to oral plea about alleged remarks by social media influencer Nazia Elahi Khan

title: What does allocation of ₹313 crore for West Bengal tea gardens mean? | Explained
content: Under the PMCSPY, ₹72 crore will be invested to upgrade health facilities and ensure robust medical services in tea garden areas

title: Prakash Raj backing YouTuber Ravan against Pawan Kalyan, allege JSP leaders
content: Party leaders Gurana Ayyalu and Avanapu Vikram alleged that Prakash Raj was instigating YouTubers against Pawan Kalyan, and that the YSRCP was funding them

title: Brain-dead 7-year-old boy’s organs to give new lease of life to six patients in Kerala
content: Lokineni Yaashvan, a native of Tirumalagiri district in Hyderabad who was residing in Tirunelveli, was hit by an ambulance while crossing the road on his bicycle on June 29 night. His six organs — both kidneys, the liver, the corneas and the heart valve — are being transplanted into patients awaiting organs for a fresh lease of life in Kerala

title: Maharashtra Council adjourned; 13 killed in rain-related incidents, says Minister
content: Girish Mahajan said Mumbai and neighbouring Palghar and Raigad districts have witnessed record-breaking rainfall, and 13 persons were killed in rain-related incidents in the last three to four days

title: Eastern India lead in GI registrations, MP records highest registration in 2025-26
content: The DPIIT Chair of WBNUJS and JHARCRAFT have secured maximum number of GI registrations for West Bengal and Jharkhand respectively.

title: Mumbai rains | Pune–Mumbai Expressway, old Mumbai–Pune Highway shut due do to landslip, heavy rainfall
content: The traffic police have issued a public appeal, requesting that citizens refrain from undertaking any journeys between Pune and Mumbai until further notice

title: Mumbai-Pune train services suspended after landslips in Karjat-Lonavala section
content: The disruptions led to the cancellation of several trains, including the CSMT-Pune Indrayani Express, Intercity Express, Deccan Express, Deccan Queen, Pragati Express and Dhule Express

title: Bypolls to three Rajya Sabha seats from West Bengal on July 24: ECI
content: The three TMC Rajya Sabha members who resigned are Sukhendu Sekhar Roy, Sushmita Dev and Prakash Chik Baraik. They stepped down on different dates in June after questioning the party leadership following its defeat in the Assembly polls

title: ‘An assault on collective memory’: Sukhbir Badal slams removal of Satluj from  OTT platform
content: “A powerful film that courageously unveils Punjab’s painful history and honours the supreme sacrifice of S. Jaswant Singh Ji Khalra cannot be silenced this way,” Sukhbir Singh Badal said

title: HMT asked to hand over 430 acres and 21 guntas of land in Peenya Plantation and Jarakabande Sandal reserve to Forest Department
content: The court of the authorized officer and Deputy Conservator of Forests, Bengaluru finds possession of land by HMT is unauthorised and illegal

title: Four, including minor, arrested for youth’s murder in Thiruvananthapuram
content: The 22-year-old victim was allegedly stabbed to death by his neighbours during a dispute that began over reckless bike racing in front of his house

title: 'MSMEs being strangled': Rahul puts out video of visit to bus, truck body-building workshop
content: Rahul Gandhi alleged that "Make in India" and "Vocal for Local" are merely slogans as the Modi Government's policies cater only to big industrialists

title: Man held after wife found dead; dowry death case registered
content: “We are examining all angles, and further investigation is under way,” said Deputy Commissioner of Police (South) Anant Mittal said

title: NFSA amendments: 7 kg of foodgrains for a person, and maximum 35 kg for a household
content: The Centre has proposed revising the foodgrain entitlement for AAY beneficiaries under the NFSA. While it says the change will ensure fairer distribution, critics fear it could reduce allocations for many households

title: TET paper-leak scam: What it’s all about, how Maharashtra Police busted it | Explained
content: The Maharashtra TET paper leak case unpacks how the examination was allegedly compromised and why investigators suspect a wider interstate network

title: Prasoon Joshi-led study group on promoting Indian cinema | Explained
content: The main issue before the Prasar Bharati chairperson-led study group is how to make Indian cinema globally competitive

title: Ketan Agarwal death case: What is gait analysis? | Explained
content: In clinical settings, gait analysis can be used to understand why someone is in pain or how a specific medical condition is progressing. Read on...

title: What are India’s problems with most credit ratings agencies? | Explained
content: India has consistently been rated just a grade or two above ‘junk’ status, which is when institutions will stop lending money for fear of default

title: Questioning the hype around Uttar Pradesh’s ‘revenue surplus’ in 10 charts
content: While Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath often highlights U.P.’s revenue surplus as a major achievement, its spending pattern shows why it may in fact be an indication of a deeper problem the developing State faces.

title: TVK challenges two provisions of T.N. government’s SOP for political rallies before Madras High Court
content: Says, they amount to colourable exercise of power by the State government in order to stifle political activities of the petitioner party

title: Karur stampede case: Supreme Court agrees to hear DMK’s plea to bar TVK leaders from ‘influencing’ material witnesses
content: DMK’s application alleged that public statements were made by TVK Minister Aadhav Arjuna against former CM M.K. Stalin in connection with the Karur stampede

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: Karur stampede yet to fade from memory of  Velusamypuram voters
content: Minutes past 2 p.m. Thursday, booth number 15 at the Panchayat Union Middle School, located a few yards away from the scene of the stampede, registered 81.31% polling, indicating that the tragedy has in no way dispirited the enthusiasm of the voters

title: Delhi Gymkhana Club served show-cause notice in eviction proceedings
content: The Estate Officer has called upon the Delhi Gymkhana Club to show-cause on or before July 7, 2026, as to why an order of eviction should not be made against it under the provisions of the Public Premises Act.

title: Delhi Gymkhana Club: A battle of public and private rights
content: Members of the club include the National Capital’s high-heeled, such as politicians, business tycoons, and celebrities.

title: IMD issues red alert for Mumbai, Thane and Raigad; urges people to avoid non-essential travel
content: The MeT office upgraded the warning from 'orange' to 'red' alert in the morning after observing changes in weather conditions

title: Heavy Mumbai rains: Three dead, four injured as rain disrupts life in Maharashtra
content: Flight operations affected for an hour at Mumbai airport; holiday declared for schools and colleges in Mumbai and Thane on July 5

title: Flashfloods block Doda-Kishtwar highway, bury vehicles at under construction Kwar power project
content: Road clearance and restoration work has been launched by authorities, while commuters have been advised to avoid the highway and follow official traffic advisories until the route is reopened.

title: Fisherman drowns off Anakapalli coast; search on for six missing off Visakhapatnam
content: Chodipalli Simhadri drowned after two of a four-member crew fell into rough seas off Revu Polavaram; the search for six missing fishermen continues off Visakhapatnam

title: Madhya Pradesh Waqf Board reorganised, Hindu members included
content: A gazette notification was issued on Sunday, July 5, appointing Sanwar Patel as the chairman of the 10-member Madhya Pradesh Waqf Board; Manoj Malpani and Animesh Bhargava have been included as Hindu members on the board.

title: Telangana DGP calls for unified department to crack down on food adulteration and narcotics
content: The extent of food adulteration in Hyderabad can be estimated from the range of products seized and unsafe handling of food uncovered by H-FAST. The proposed State-level department is expected to build on H-FAST’s model.

title: HC seeks Centre's stand on plea of Gymkhana members against show-cause notice for eviction
content: Justice Avneesh Jhingan issued notice to the Central Government on applications seeking a stay on the operation of the show-cause notice and asked Solicitor General Tushar Mehta

title: AI-powered tools can trace missing persons faster, with more accuracy, say experts
content: They can significantly improve speed and accuracy of investigations, especially when time is critical

title: T.K. Govindan accuses M.V. Jayarajan of conspiracy to tarnish CPI(M) Kerala secretary M.V. Govindan’s image
content: T.K. Govindan rules out possibility of returning to CPI(M), saying he will not join any political party

title: Andhra Pradesh introduces millet ‘chikki’ in government schools to boost student nutrition
content: The new ‘chikki’ combines peanuts, blended millets and jaggery syrup, making it richer in dietary fibre and calcium, and will be served to students three times a week

title: 14 stalled bridges from Anakapalli to Pulivendula to be completed with ₹90.20 crore
content: Of the 14 bridges, stalled for years mostly over land acquisition, 11 stand built but unusable for want of approach roads, Roads and Buildings Minister B.C. Janardhan Reddy said

title: Iran’s move to invite Sunni Kashmir leader Mehbooba conveys a message
content: Mehbooba Mufti attending supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s final rites is bound to change the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) president’s status in politics now onwards

title: PDP chief Mehbooba Mufti, five Shia clerics in J&K get Iran’s invite to Ali Khamenei’s funeral
content: The PDP chief is likely to visit Tehran “to pay her respects and offer condolences” to the people and the leadership of the Islamic Republic of Iran, party sources said

title: The real crisis in India’s fisheries
content: Official claims that most of India’s marine fish stocks are sustainable should not distract from the continuing degradation of the country’s inshore fishing grounds; stronger governance of coastal waters and better management of mechanised trawling are essential for truly sustainable fisheries

title: Pidari Ekaveeri: a fierce Goddess, a captivating smile, and Raja Raja’s mother-in-law’s grant
content: R. Kalaikovan of the Dr. Rajamanickanar Centre for Historical Research says the idols is the probably oldest identifiable Pidari image in Tamil Nadu. Although several inscriptions from the Chola period refer to Pidari deities, the corresponding images have rarely been identified, he adds

title: Two, including 8-year-old girl, injured in firing by armed groups in Manipur’s Kangpokpi
content: The injured were initially provided first aid at an Assam Rifles camp and later brought to the Regional Institute of Medical Sciences in Imphal for further treatment

title: Crucial Ram temple trust meeting today; treasurer distances himself from donation row
content: Ahead of the meeting, Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust treasurer Govind Dev Giri distanced himself from the controversy, saying he had no role in the daily donation process

title: A majority of departments are yet to upload G.O.s on the Tamil Nadu government website
content: Only five departments have hosted their G.O.s issued in May and June on the website. The orders that are not available on the site pertain to the creation of Singappen Special Force and the provision of 100 additional units of electricity to domestic consumers who use 500 units bi-monthly

title: How are terrorists designated under the UAPA? | Explained
content: What is the law under which terrorist designations taken place? Who were the first individuals to be designated terrorists? What is the process involved?

title: AMMA crisis: Shwetha Menon defends her presidency, claims support from superstars
content: ‘Those who wish to be part of the AMMA committee should do so by contesting elections as we did, rather than attempt entry through the back door,’ she says in a Facebook post

title: "Satluj Sympathises With Terrorism": Lawyer Seeks FIR Against Diljit Dosanjh
content: Originally titled 'Punjab 95', the long-delayed movie was released on ZEE5 under the title Satluj last Friday but was taken down soon after.

title: Weather Today LIVE: 17 Flights Cancelled, 217 Delayed At Mumbai Airport Amid Heavy Rain
content: Monsoon Alert Live Updates: The disruptions occurred amid the ongoing monsoon season, when periods of intense rainfall and gusty winds frequently impact air travel, particularly on high-density routes such as the Delhi-Mumbai corridor.

title: Two Men Drown At Navi Mumbai Dam Complex As Heavy Rain Swells Water
content: So far, the body of one person has been recovered, while a police search operation is underway for the other.

title: Missing Census, Poor Maintenance: Experts On Factors Behind Delhi Tree Falls
content: Experts recommend conducting a tree census and health audits for early detection and treatment of the tree's issues.

title: 3 Dead In Pune Landslides, Massive Traffic Congestion On Mumbai-Pune Highway
content: The massive landslide has also severely affected traffic movement in the area. Traffic on both the Mumbai-Pune Expressway and the old Mumbai-Pune Highway was halted on Monday

title: Indigenous Rifle Made In 100 Days Clears Tests, Set For CAPF Induction
content: The rifle has successfully cleared Army General Staff Qualitative Requirement (GSQR) trials, extensive field evaluations in all climatic conditions and MHA Board trials.

title: In Clash Over Telangana Irrigation Project, A Drought Warning, And Blame Game
content: KTR has demanded that the Congress government in Telangana immediately begin lifting Godavari waters from the Kannepalli Pump House.

title: Flash Floods In J&K Bury Machinery At Power Project Site, Block Highway
content: A sudden flash flood turned into a moving mountain of water, mud, and house-sized boulders in a matter of minutes that slammed into the under-construction 540 MW Kwar Hydroelectric Project in Kishtwar.

title: Arunachal Government Launches Emergency Helicopter Op For Flood Relief
content: Arunachal Pradesh uses helicopters to supply food and evacuate flood-affected villages in Lower Siang district

title: "Could Be Misused By Anti-India Forces": Sources On Why 'Satluj' Was Pulled Down
content: Originally named 'Punjab 95', the movie was released on ZEE5 under the new title Satluj on July 3, after years of delay and controversies.

title: Meghalaya Seeks Relaxations In Foreign Funding Rules For Schools, Hospitals
content: A delegation led by the chief minister said several charitable institutions in Meghalaya rely on foreign contributions to run their activities.

title: 116-Year-Old Devotee Gets VIP Darshan At Tirumala After Viral Walking Video
content: Despite her advanced age, she climbed the nearly 3550 steps along the 9-km Alipiri footpath through the Seshachalam hills to offer prayers to Lord Venkateswara.

title: 'Gratitude To Friendly India': Iran's Thank You Note Amid Khamenei's Funeral
content: In a long post on X, the Iranian Embassy thanked the Indian community for participation and said that the Islamic republic will never forget this "gesture of friendship, compassion, and heartfelt respect."

title: 'Restraining Order Needed': Trump Reignites Feud With Italian PM Meloni Ahead Of NATO Summit
content: The feud between the two world leaders began when Trump claimed last week that Meloni asked "over and over" for a photo with him during the recent Group of Seven summit -- and suggested she begged for such a picture.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Satluj, Jaswant Singh Khalra, And When Disappearance Is The Story
content: Jaswant Singh Khalra followed the dead to restore their dignity. Three decades later, a film on his life briefly appeared and then disappeared from Indian screens.

title: 'Have some other friends, like India': Netanyahu counters Vance's 'only ally' claim
